ADDM Task ADDM:2951140681_2_50294

          ADDM Report for Task 'ADDM:2951140681_2_50294'
          ----------------------------------------------

Analysis Period
---------------
AWR snapshot range from 50293 to 50294.
Time period starts at 01-APR-26 11.00.03 PM
Time period ends at 02-APR-26 12.00.43 AM

Analysis Target
---------------
Database 'DWS19C' with DB ID 2951140681.
Database version during analysis period was 19.24.0.0.0.
Database version when ADDM was executed was 19.0.0.0.0.
All recommendations generated by ADDM are valid for database version 19.0.0.0.0, the version in which ADDM was executed.
ADDM performed an analysis of instance dws2, numbered 2 and hosted at
sjjhdb02.

Activity During the Analysis Period
-----------------------------------
Total database time was 287111 seconds.
The average number of active sessions was 78.88.

Summary of Findings
-------------------
   Description                               Active Sessions      Recommendations
                                             Percent of Activity
   ----------------------------------------  -------------------  ---------------
1  Top SQL Statements                        70.4 | 89.25         5
2  PL/SQL Execution                          9.33 | 11.83         2
3  Buffer Busy - Hot Objects                 5.37 | 6.81          1
4  Undersized PGA                            4.73 | 6             1
5  Temp Space Contention                     4.04 | 5.12          1
6  ITL Waits                                 3.09 | 3.92          6
7  Top Segments by "User I/O" and "Cluster"  2.29 | 2.91          1
8  Undersized SGA                            2.17 | 2.76          1


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~


          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 70.4 active sessions, 89.25% of total activity.
---------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 29.43 active sessions, 37.31% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 90% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was executed 8 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 13824 seconds.
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on
      the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 5fazzyb6hs9g6.
         call DBMS_PARALLEL_EXECUTE.RUN_INTERNAL_WORKER ( :0,:1 )

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 12.52 active sessions, 15.87% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the CREATE INDEX statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 9rtbj6mb2dn0a.
         alter index CEN_CORE_V30.PK_ZL2303 rebuild nologging parallel 32
         storage(initial 1m next 1m)
   Rationale
      The SQL Tuning Advisor cannot operate on CREATE INDEX statements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "buffer busy waits" in wait class "Concurrency"
      accounted for 30% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a".
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "enq: SS - contention" in wait class "Configuration"
      accounted for 30% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a".

   Recommendation 3: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 10.2 active sessions, 12.93% of total activity.
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the CREATE INDEX statement with SQL_ID "fy1vygbq261fu" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID fy1vygbq261fu.
         alter index CEN_CORE_V30.PK_YY1002 rebuild nologging parallel 32
         storage(initial 1m next 1m)
   Rationale
      The SQL Tuning Advisor cannot operate on CREATE INDEX statements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.

   Recommendation 4: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 9.73 active sessions, 12.33% of total activity.
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "2ra448fasau1h".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 2ra448fasau1h.
         merge into area_xsjk.tb_qsqsn_jzl a
         using(
         with patient_info as(
         select *
         from cen_core_v30.tb_patient_information_x x
         where Fun_ChkCardId(x.zjhm) = '01'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and length(zjhm)=18),
         mzjzl as
         (select ywrq, nl, count(1) mzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq,
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4) nl,
         a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record a
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '1' -- ĂĹŐď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) between '3'
         and '18'))
         group by ywrq, nl),
         --Ľ±Őď
         jzjzl as
         (select ywrq, nl, count(1) jzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq,
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4) nl,
         a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record a
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '2' -- Ľ±Őď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) between '3'
         and '18'))
         group by ywrq, nl),
         -- ×ˇÔş
         zyjzl as
         (select ywrq, nl, count(1) zysl
         from (select
         to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq,
         to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4) nl,
         a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_zy_medical_record a
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.RYSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) between '3' and
         '18'))
         group by ywrq, nl),
         -- ĘÖĘő TB_Operation_Detail
         ssjzl as
         (select ywrq, nl, count(1) sssl
         from (select
         to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq,       --ĘÖĘő˝áĘřĘ±Ľä×÷ÎŞŇµÎńĘ±Ľä
         to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4) nl,
         a.*
         from cen_core_v30.TB_Operation_Detail a
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.SSJSSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) between '3'
         and '18'))
         group by ywrq, nl)
         select /*+parallel(32)*/ ywrq,nl,max(mzsl) mzsl,max(jzsl)
         jzsl,max(zysl) zysl,max(sssl) sssl,sysdate jlgxsj
         from(
         select ywrq,nl,mzsl,0 jzsl,0 zysl,0 sssl  from mzjzl a
         union all
         select ywrq,nl,0,jzsl,0,0  from jzjzl a
         union all
         select ywrq,nl,0,0,zysl,0  from zyjzl a
         union all
         select ywrq,nl,0,0,0,sssl  from ssjzl a
         )
         group by ywrq,nl
         ) b
         on (a.ywrq = b.ywrq and a.nl = b.nl)
         when matched then
         update set a.mzjzl = b.mzsl, a.jzjzl = b.jzsl, a.zyl = b.zysl, a.ssl
         = b.sssl, a.jlgxsj = b.jlgxsj
         when not matched then
         insert (A.YWRQ, A.NL, A.MZJZL, A.JZJZL, A.ZYL, A.SSL, A.JLGXSJ)
         VALUES (B.YWRQ, B.NL, B.mzsl, B.jzsl, B.ZYSL, B.SSSL, B.JLGXSJ)
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 99% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 59% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 41% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "2ra448fasau1h" was executed 1 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 33969 seconds.
   Rationale
      At least one execution of the statement ran in parallel.

   Recommendation 5: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 5.3 active sessions, 6.72% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "gsmzsg9hd1ycy".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID gsmzsg9hd1ycy.
         merge into area_xsjk.TB_QSN_ZLQK a
         using(
         with patient_info as(
         select *
         from cen_core_v30.tb_patient_information_x x
         where Fun_ChkCardId(x.zjhm) = '01'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and length(zjhm)=18),
         --ĂĹŐď
         mzjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) mzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '1' -- ĂĹŐď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         --Ľ±Őď
         jzjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) jzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '2' -- Ľ±Őď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         -- ×ˇÔş
         zyjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) zysl
         from (select
         to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_zy_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.RYSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         -- ĘÖĘő TB_Operation_Detail
         ssjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) sssl
         from (select
         to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.TB_Operation_Detail  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.SSJSSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc)
         select /*+parallel(32)*/  ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc,max(mzsl)
         mzsl,max(jzsl) jzsl,max(zysl) zysl,max(sssl) sssl,sysdate jlgxsj
         from(
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, mzsl, 0 jzsl, 0 zysl, 0 sssl from
         mzjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, jzsl, 0, 0 from jzjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, 0, zysl, 0 from zyjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, 0, 0, sssl from ssjzl
         )
         group by ywrq,fj_yljgdm,fj_yljgmc
         ) b
         on (a.ywrq = b.ywrq and a.YLJGDM = b.fj_yljgdm)
         when matched then
         update set a.mzjzl = b.mzsl, a.jzjzl = b.jzsl, a.zyl = b.zysl, a.ssl
         = b.sssl, a.jlgxsj = b.jlgxsj
         when not matched then
         insert  (A.YWRQ, A.YLJGDM, A.YLJGMC, A.MZJZL, A.JZJZL, A.ZYL, A.SSL,
         A.JLGXSJ)
         VALUES  (B.YWRQ, B.FJ_YLJGDM, B.FJ_YLJGMC, B.MZSL, B.JZSL, B.ZYSL,
         B.SSSL, B.JLGXSJ)
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 100% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 25% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 75% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      At least one execution of the statement ran in parallel.


Finding 2: PL/SQL Execution
Impact is 9.33 active sessions, 11.83% of total activity.
---------------------------------------------------------
PL/SQL execution consumed significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 7.67 active sessions, 9.72% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "CEN_ETL_JYGL.FUN_CHKCARDID" of type
      "FUNCTION" and ID 5717890. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      27905 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL "CEN_ETL_JYGL.FUN_CHKCARDID" of
      type "FUNCTION" and ID 5717890.

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.66 active sessions, 2.11% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS"
      of type "PACKAGE" and ID 7169. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      6046 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS" of type "PACKAGE" and ID
      7169.


Finding 3: Buffer Busy - Hot Objects
Impact is 5.37 active sessions, 6.81% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, .03% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Trace the cause of contention on INDEX "SYS.LINK_LOGONS$_SRCID_IDX" with
      object ID 10530 in the application.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 10530.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
      database time.
      Impact is 5.37 active sessions, 6.81% of total activity.
         Wait class "Concurrency" was consuming significant database time.
         Impact is 4.26 active sessions, 5.4% of total activity.
         Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on
         this instance.
         Impact is 1.1 active sessions, 1.4% of total activity.
            Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
            Impact is 2.09 active sessions, 2.65% of total activity.


Finding 4: Undersized PGA
Impact is 4.73 active sessions, 6% of total activity.
-----------------------------------------------------
The PGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O to temporary
tablespaces to consume significant database time.
The value of parameter "pga_aggregate_target" was "51712 M" during the
analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .14 active sessions, .18% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the PGA by setting the value of parameter
      "pga_aggregate_target" to 72181 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 12.36 active sessions, 15.67% of total activity.


Finding 5: Temp Space Contention
Impact is 4.04 active sessions, 5.12% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Contention for space in temporary tablespace by multiple instances was
consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is 4.04 active sessions, 5.12% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing the size of the temporary tablespaces that are
      experiencing significant inter-instance contention.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Configuration" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 7.51 active sessions, 9.53% of total activity.


Finding 6: ITL Waits
Impact is 3.09 active sessions, 3.92% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Waits for ITL enqueue were consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is 1.34 active sessions, 1.7% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing INITRANS for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073 without rebuilding it.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037073.
   Action
      Consider increasing PCTFREE for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037073.
   Rationale
      The SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was found waiting for the
      Interested Transaction List (ITL) enqueue on the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2

   Recommendation 2: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is 1.34 active sessions, 1.7% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider rebuilding the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object id
      4037073 using a higher value for INITRANS.

         Database object with ID 4037073.

Finding 7: Top Segments by "User I/O" and "Cluster"
Impact is 2.29 active sessions, 2.91% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Individual database segments responsible for significant "User I/O" and
"Cluster" waits were found.

   Recommendation 1: Segment Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 2.29 active sessions, 2.91% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic involving I/O on INDEX
      "CEN_CORE_V30.PK_YY1002" with object ID 4232210.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4232210.
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant I/O on this segment. For example, the CREATE INDEX statement
      with SQL_ID "fy1vygbq261fu" is responsible for 100% of "User I/O" and
      "Cluster" waits for this segment.
   Rationale
      The I/O usage statistics for the object are: 30 full object scans,
      27311758 physical reads, 31550748 physical writes and 27186140 direct
      reads.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 12.36 active sessions, 15.67% of total activity.


Finding 8: Undersized SGA
Impact is 2.17 active sessions, 2.76% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .49 active sessions, .62% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the SGA by setting the parameter "sga_target" to
      294912 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 12.36 active sessions, 15.67% of total activity.

Miscellaneous Information
-------------------------
Wait class "Application" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Commit" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Session connect and disconnect calls were not consuming significant database
time.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 63.77 active sessions, 86.99% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 29.92 active sessions, 40.81% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 92% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was executed 17 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 6398 seconds.
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on
      the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 5fazzyb6hs9g6.
         call DBMS_PARALLEL_EXECUTE.RUN_INTERNAL_WORKER ( :0,:1 )

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 25.92 active sessions, 35.35% of total activity.
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the CREATE INDEX statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 9rtbj6mb2dn0a.
         alter index CEN_CORE_V30.PK_ZL2303 rebuild nologging parallel 32
         storage(initial 1m next 1m)
   Rationale
      The SQL Tuning Advisor cannot operate on CREATE INDEX statements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      At least one execution of the statement ran in parallel.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "enq: SS - contention" in wait class "Configuration"
      accounted for 18% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a".
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "buffer busy waits" in wait class "Concurrency"
      accounted for 15% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a".

   Recommendation 3: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 2.06 active sessions, 2.81% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the CREATE TABLE statement with SQL_ID
      "8pjnzhhaq3nv1".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 8pjnzhhaq3nv1.
         CREATE TABLE cen_core_jygl.tmp_jm_0002_1  parallel 32 nologging AS
         SELECT
         /*+parallel(a,32) */ /*+parallel(b,32) */ /*+parallel(c,32) */
         c.wsjgdm, a.yljgdm, a.kh, a.klx, a.zzysgh gh,
         COUNT(DISTINCT a.jzlsh) mzcs,
         SUM(CASE
         WHEN stfbz = '1' THEN
         stfze
         ELSE
         -stfze
         END) zfy, c.jgdj
         FROM   cen_core_jygl.tb_yl_mz_medical_record a
         JOIN   cen_core_jygl.tb_his_mz_charge b
         ON     (a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm AND a.jzlsh = b.jzlsh)
         JOIN   (SELECT DISTINCT yljgdm, wsjgdm, jgdj
         FROM   cen_core_jygl.t_dic_jg_kz) c
         ON     (a.yljgdm = c.yljgdm)
         WHERE  to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymm') >= '202501'
         AND    to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymm') <='202604'
         AND    a.klx = '0'
         AND    a.jzlx NOT IN ('2', '6')
         AND    a.xgbz = '1'
         AND    length(a.kh) = 9
         GROUP  BY c.wsjgdm, a.yljgdm, a.kh, a.klx, a.zzysgh, c.jgdj
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 100% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.

   Recommendation 4: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.81 active sessions, 2.47% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "dgzq1t8652p8z".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID dgzq1t8652p8z.
         merge into area_xsjk.TB_HCV_JCYX m
         using(
         select ywrq,count(1) rs
         from(
         select /*+parallel(4)*/ distinct substr(a.bgrq,1,4) ywrq,b.kh,b.klx
         from cen_core_v30.tb_lis_indicators a
         join cen_core_v30.tb_lis_report b
         on a.bgdh = b.bgdh and a.bgrq = b.bgrq and a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         where a.ybsfdmyb in ('S250403014a0010','S250403014b0010')
         AND A.JCZBJG LIKE '%Ńô%'
         AND A.XGBZ = '1'
         AND SUBSTR(A.BGRQ,1,4)= to_char(sysdate,'yyyy'))
         group by ywrq
         ) t
         on (m.ywrq = t.ywrq)
         when matched then
         update set m.rs = t.rs, m.jlgxsj = sysdate
         when not matched then
         insert (m.ywrq,m.rs)
         values (t.ywrq,t.rs)
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 100% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.

   Recommendation 5: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.65 active sessions, 2.26% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "gsmzsg9hd1ycy".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID gsmzsg9hd1ycy.
         merge into area_xsjk.TB_QSN_ZLQK a
         using(
         with patient_info as(
         select *
         from cen_core_v30.tb_patient_information_x x
         where Fun_ChkCardId(x.zjhm) = '01'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and length(zjhm)=18),
         --ĂĹŐď
         mzjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) mzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '1' -- ĂĹŐď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         --Ľ±Őď
         jzjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) jzsl
         from (select
         to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_mz_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.jzksrq,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and substr(a.jzlx, 1, 1) = '2' -- Ľ±Őď
         and ((to_char(a.jzksrq, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         -- ×ˇÔş
         zyjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) zysl
         from (select
         to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.tb_yl_zy_medical_record  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.RYSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.RYSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc),
         -- ĘÖĘő TB_Operation_Detail
         ssjzl as
         (select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, count(1) sssl
         from (select
         to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyymmdd') ywrq, b.fj_yljgdm, b.fj_yljgmc, a.*
         from cen_core_v30.TB_Operation_Detail  a
         join cendq_v30.tb_yljgdm_comparison b
         on a.yljgdm = b.yljgdm
         left join patient_info x
         on a.kh = x.kh
         and a.klx = x.klx
         where a.xgbz = '1'
         and x.xgbz = '1'
         and (to_char(a.SSJSSJ,'yyyymmdd') between '20260301' and '20260401')
         and ((to_char(a.SSJSSJ, 'yyyy') - substr(x.zjhm, 7, 4)) < '18')
         and b.fj_yljgdm in
         ('42500651201', '42500244X00', '42507230900', '42502659900'))
         group by ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc)
         select /*+parallel(32)*/  ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc,max(mzsl)
         mzsl,max(jzsl) jzsl,max(zysl) zysl,max(sssl) sssl,sysdate jlgxsj
         from(
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, mzsl, 0 jzsl, 0 zysl, 0 sssl from
         mzjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, jzsl, 0, 0 from jzjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, 0, zysl, 0 from zyjzl
         union all
         select ywrq, fj_yljgdm, fj_yljgmc, 0, 0, 0, sssl from ssjzl
         )
         group by ywrq,fj_yljgdm,fj_yljgmc
         ) b
         on (a.ywrq = b.ywrq and a.YLJGDM = b.fj_yljgdm)
         when matched then
         update set a.mzjzl = b.mzsl, a.jzjzl = b.jzsl, a.zyl = b.zysl, a.ssl
         = b.sssl, a.jlgxsj = b.jlgxsj
         when not matched then
         insert  (A.YWRQ, A.YLJGDM, A.YLJGMC, A.MZJZL, A.JZJZL, A.ZYL, A.SSL,
         A.JLGXSJ)
         VALUES  (B.YWRQ, B.FJ_YLJGDM, B.FJ_YLJGMC, B.MZSL, B.JZSL, B.ZYSL,
         B.SSSL, B.JLGXSJ)
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 100% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "gsmzsg9hd1ycy" was executed 1 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 6255 seconds.
   Rationale
      At least one execution of the statement ran in parallel.


Finding 2: Buffer Busy - Hot Objects
Impact is 5.5 active sessions, 7.51% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
database time.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .03 active sessions, .03% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Trace the cause of contention on TABLE "SYS.LINK_LOGONS$" with object ID
      10529 in the application.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 10529.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
      database time.
      Impact is 5.51 active sessions, 7.51% of total activity.
         Wait class "Concurrency" was consuming significant database time.
         Impact is 4.61 active sessions, 6.29% of total activity.
         Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on
         this instance.
         Impact is .84 active sessions, 1.14% of total activity.
            Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
            Impact is 1.82 active sessions, 2.49% of total activity.


Finding 3: Temp Space Contention
Impact is 4.94 active sessions, 6.74% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Contention for space in temporary tablespace by multiple instances was
consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is 4.94 active sessions, 6.74% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing the size of the temporary tablespaces that are
      experiencing significant inter-instance contention.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Configuration" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 8.08 active sessions, 11.02% of total activity.


Finding 4: Undersized PGA
Impact is 4.89 active sessions, 6.66% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The PGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O to temporary
tablespaces to consume significant database time.
The value of parameter "pga_aggregate_target" was "51712 M" during the
analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .15 active sessions, .2% of total activity.
   ----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the PGA by setting the value of parameter
      "pga_aggregate_target" to 61869 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 16.89 active sessions, 23.04% of total activity.


Finding 5: Top Segments by "User I/O" and "Cluster"
Impact is 4.69 active sessions, 6.4% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Individual database segments responsible for significant "User I/O" and
"Cluster" waits were found.

   Recommendation 1: Segment Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 4.7 active sessions, 6.4% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic involving I/O on INDEX
      "CEN_CORE_V30.PK_ZL2303" with object ID 4232706.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4232706.
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant I/O on this segment. For example, the CREATE INDEX statement
      with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a" is responsible for 100% of "User I/O" and
      "Cluster" waits for this segment.
   Rationale
      The I/O usage statistics for the object are: 0 full object scans,
      28693202 physical reads, 60698182 physical writes and 28693458 direct
      reads.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 16.89 active sessions, 23.04% of total activity.


Finding 6: ITL Waits
Impact is 2.4 active sessions, 3.27% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Waits for ITL enqueue were consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is 1.49 active sessions, 2.03% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing INITRANS for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_44" with object ID
      4037066 without rebuilding it.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037066.
   Action
      Consider increasing PCTFREE for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_44" with object ID
      4037066.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037066.
   Rationale
      The SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was found waiting for the
      Interested Transaction List (ITL) enqueue on the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_44" with object ID
      4037066.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2

   Recommendation 2: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is 1.49 active sessions, 2.03% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action

      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_44" with object id
      4037066 using a higher value for INITRANS.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037066.

Finding 7: PL/SQL Execution
Impact is 2.08 active sessions, 2.83% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
PL/SQL execution consumed significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.52 active sessions, 2.08% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS"
      of type "PACKAGE" and ID 7169. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      5457 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS" of type "PACKAGE" and ID
      7169.

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .29 active sessions, .39% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "CENDOC.SP_JKDA_ALL" of type "PROCEDURE" and
      ID 3202007. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition information.
   Rationale
      971 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "CENDOC.ZH_CONCAT_IM.ODCIAGGREGATETERMINATE" of type "TYPE" and ID
      3200924.

   Recommendation 3: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .25 active sessions, .33% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "CEN_ETL_JYGL.IS_SFZ" of type "FUNCTION" and
      ID 5246483. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition information.
   Rationale
      878 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL "CEN_ETL_JYGL.IS_SFZ" of type
      "FUNCTION" and ID 5246483.

   Recommendation 4: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .02 active sessions, .03% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_REFRESH.REFRESH" of type "PACKAGE"
      and ID 15401. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition
      information.
   Rationale
      Subprogam ID 1 of PL/SQL ID 0 spent 82 seconds in execution.


Finding 8: Undersized SGA
Impact is 2.02 active sessions, 2.75% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .23 active sessions, .31% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the SGA by setting the parameter "sga_target" to
      278528 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 16.89 active sessions, 23.04% of total activity.


Finding 9: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is .84 active sessions, 1.14% of total activity.
-------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .84 active sessions, 1.14% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant time on Cluster waits. For example, the CREATE INDEX
      statement with SQL_ID "9rtbj6mb2dn0a" is responsible for 100% of Cluster
      wait during the analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 1.82 active sessions, 2.49% of total activity.

          Additional Information
          ----------------------

Miscellaneous Information
-------------------------
Wait class "Application" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Commit" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Session connect and disconnect calls were not consuming significant database
time.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.

          Findings and Recommendations
          ----------------------------

Finding 1: Top SQL Statements
Impact is 65.65 active sessions, 90.86% of total activity.
----------------------------------------------------------
SQL statements consuming significant database time were found. These
statements offer a good opportunity for performance improvement.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 27.8 active sessions, 38.48% of total activity.
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 85% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was executed 11 times and had
      an average elapsed time of 10027 seconds.
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on
      the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 5fazzyb6hs9g6.
         call DBMS_PARALLEL_EXECUTE.RUN_INTERNAL_WORKER ( :0,:1 )

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 19.15 active sessions, 26.5% of total activity.
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate the CREATE INDEX statement with SQL_ID "7fw1pprzuj4dx" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7fw1pprzuj4dx.
         alter index CEN_CORE_V30.PK_BL0002 rebuild nologging parallel 32
         storage(initial 1m next 1m)
   Rationale
      The SQL Tuning Advisor cannot operate on CREATE INDEX statements.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      Waiting for event "enq: SS - contention" in wait class "Configuration"
      accounted for 21% of the database time spent in processing the SQL
      statement with SQL_ID "7fw1pprzuj4dx".

   Recommendation 3: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 5.83 active sessions, 8.08% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------

      Investigate the CREATE INDEX statement with SQL_ID "bm4a8puv5qjf9" for
      possible performance improvements. You can supplement the information
      given here with an ASH report for this SQL_ID.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID bm4a8puv5qjf9.
         alter index CEN_CORE_V30.IX_BL02_JG0002 rebuild nologging parallel 32
         storage(initial 1m next 1m)

      The SQL Tuning Advisor cannot operate on CREATE INDEX statements.

      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.

      SQL statement with SQL_ID "918x5p2hdv7n8" was executed 1466 times and

      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on

   Recommendation 5: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.88 active sessions, 2.61% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Run SQL Tuning Advisor on the UPSERT statement with SQL_ID
      "cq9v2x1zxdu4g".
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID cq9v2x1zxdu4g.
         MERGE INTO CEN_CORE_V30.TB_HIS_ZY_FEE_DETAIL A USING (SELECT
         WSJGDM,SFMXID,XGBZ,FJ_WSJGDM,YLJGDM,JZLSH,KH,KLX,FPH,BXLX,MXFYLB,STFS
         J,MXXMBMYB,SCPH,YXQZ,MXXMMC,MXXMDW,MXXMDJ,MXXMSL,MXXMJE,YLYL1,YLYL2,J
         LSCRQ,POSID FROM CEN_INTERFACE_V30.TB_HIS_ZY_FEE_DETAIL C WHERE ROWID
         BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1 ) B ON (A.YLJGDM=B.YLJGDM AND A.SFMXID=B.SFMXID
         AND A.XGBZ=B.XGBZ) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.WSJGDM=
         B.WSJGDM,A.FJ_WSJGDM= B.FJ_WSJGDM,A.JZLSH= B.JZLSH,A.KH= B.KH,A.KLX=
         B.KLX,A.FPH= B.FPH,A.BXLX= B.BXLX,A.MXFYLB= B.MXFYLB,A.STFSJ=
         B.STFSJ,A.MXXMBMYB= B.MXXMBMYB,A.SCPH= B.SCPH,A.YXQZ=
         B.YXQZ,A.MXXMMC= B.MXXMMC,A.MXXMDW= B.MXXMDW,A.MXXMDJ=
         B.MXXMDJ,A.MXXMSL= B.MXXMSL,A.MXXMJE= B.MXXMJE,A.YLYL1=
         B.YLYL1,A.YLYL2= B.YLYL2 , A.JLSCRQ= B.JLSCRQ, A.POSID= B.POSID,
         A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
         INSERT(WSJGDM,SFMXID,XGBZ,FJ_WSJGDM,YLJGDM,JZLSH,KH,KLX,FPH,BXLX,MXFY
         LB,STFSJ,MXXMBMYB,SCPH,YXQZ,MXXMMC,MXXMDW,MXXMDJ,MXXMSL,MXXMJE,YLYL1,
         YLYL2,JLSCRQ,POSID) VALUES(
         B.WSJGDM,B.SFMXID,B.XGBZ,B.FJ_WSJGDM,B.YLJGDM,B.JZLSH,B.KH,B.KLX,B.FP
         H,B.BXLX,B.MXFYLB,B.STFSJ,B.MXXMBMYB,B.SCPH,B.YXQZ,B.MXXMMC,B.MXXMDW,
         B.MXXMDJ,B.MXXMSL,B.MXXMJE,B.YLYL1,B.YLYL2,B.JLSCRQ,B.POSID)
   Rationale
      The SQL spent 81% of its database time on CPU, I/O and Cluster waits.
      This part of database time may be improved by the SQL Tuning Advisor.
   Rationale
      Database time for this SQL was divided as follows: 100% for SQL
      execution, 0% for parsing, 0% for PL/SQL execution and 0% for Java
      execution.
   Rationale
      SQL statement with SQL_ID "cq9v2x1zxdu4g" was executed 1495 times and
      had an average elapsed time of 5.5 seconds.
   Rationale
      Top level calls to execute the PL/SQL statement with SQL_ID
      "5fazzyb6hs9g6" are responsible for 100% of the database time spent on

Finding 2: Buffer Busy - Hot Objects
Impact is 6.75 active sessions, 9.34% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
database time.

   Recommendation 1: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is .78 active sessions, 1.08% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider rebuilding the TABLE "CENDOC.TMP_20220127" with object ID
      4378874 using a higher value for PCTFREE.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4378874.

   Recommendation 2: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is .57 active sessions, .78% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Trace the cause of contention on INDEX "CEN_CORE_V30.IX_CF02_ZL2403"
      with object ID 4232744 in the application.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4232744.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Read and write contention on database blocks was consuming significant
      database time.
      Impact is 6.75 active sessions, 9.34% of total activity.
         Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on
         this instance.
         Impact is 5.32 active sessions, 7.37% of total activity.
            Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
            Impact is 6.66 active sessions, 9.22% of total activity.
         Wait class "Concurrency" was consuming significant database time.
         Impact is 1.9 active sessions, 2.63% of total activity.


Finding 3: Top Segments by "User I/O" and "Cluster"
Impact is 6.53 active sessions, 9.04% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Individual database segments responsible for significant "User I/O" and
"Cluster" waits were found.

   Recommendation 1: Segment Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 3.64 active sessions, 5.04% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic involving I/O on INDEX
      "CEN_INTERFACE_V30.IX_S_ZL2403" with object ID 4004876.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4004876.
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant I/O on this segment. For example, the DELETE statement with
      SQL_ID "918x5p2hdv7n8" is responsible for 100% of "User I/O" and
      "Cluster" waits for this segment.
   Rationale
      The I/O usage statistics for the object are: 1503 full object scans,
      90868 physical reads, 2104944 physical writes and 0 direct reads.

   Recommendation 2: Segment Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 2.89 active sessions, 4% of total activity.
   ----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Investigate application logic involving I/O on INDEX
      "CEN_CORE_V30.PK_BL0002" with object ID 4005014.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4005014.
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant I/O on this segment. For example, the CREATE INDEX statement
      with SQL_ID "7fw1pprzuj4dx" is responsible for 100% of "User I/O" and
      "Cluster" waits for this segment.
   Rationale
      The I/O usage statistics for the object are: 58 full object scans,
      59998277 physical reads, 37075426 physical writes and 59646431 direct
      reads.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 11.66 active sessions, 16.14% of total activity.


Finding 4: Global Cache Messaging
Impact is 5.32 active sessions, 7.37% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Inter-instance messaging was consuming significant database time on this
instance.

   Recommendation 1: Application Analysis
   Estimated benefit is 5.32 active sessions, 7.37% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Look at the "Top SQL Statements" finding for SQL statements consuming
      significant time on Cluster waits. For example, the DELETE statement
      with SQL_ID "918x5p2hdv7n8" is responsible for 68% of Cluster wait
      during the analysis period.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Cluster" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 6.66 active sessions, 9.22% of total activity.


Finding 5: ITL Waits
Impact is 4.72 active sessions, 6.53% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Waits for ITL enqueue were consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Schema Changes
   Estimated benefit is 3.19 active sessions, 4.41% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing INITRANS for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073 without rebuilding it.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037073.
   Action
      Consider increasing PCTFREE for the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073.
      Related Object
         Database object with ID 4037073.
   Rationale
      The SQL statement with SQL_ID "7s1rd38q2qwbj" was found waiting for the
      Interested Transaction List (ITL) enqueue on the TABLE PARTITION
      "CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X.P_RANGE_51" with object ID
      4037073.
      Related Object
         SQL statement with SQL_ID 7s1rd38q2qwbj.
         MERGE INTO (SELECT * FROM CEN_CORE_V30.TB_PATIENT_INFORMATION_X X
         WHERE LLBZ = 2) A USING (SELECT * FROM
         CEN_ETL_V30.TMP_EMPI_02GETNEWDAH Y WHERE Y.ROWID BETWEEN :B2 AND :B1
         ) B ON (A.CSDAH = B.CSDAH) WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET A.DAH =
         B.NEW_DAH, A.JLGXSJ = SYSDATE, A.LLBZ = 0 WHERE A.LLBZ = 2

Finding 6: Undersized PGA
Impact is 4.52 active sessions, 6.25% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The PGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O to temporary
tablespaces to consume significant database time.
The value of parameter "pga_aggregate_target" was "51712 M" during the
analysis period.

   No recommendations are available.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 11.66 active sessions, 16.14% of total activity.


Finding 7: Temp Space Contention
Impact is 4.24 active sessions, 5.87% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
Contention for space in temporary tablespace by multiple instances was
consuming significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is 4.24 active sessions, 5.87% of total activity.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Consider increasing the size of the temporary tablespaces that are
      experiencing significant inter-instance contention.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "Configuration" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 9.62 active sessions, 13.31% of total activity.


Finding 8: Undersized SGA
Impact is 1.98 active sessions, 2.75% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
The SGA was inadequately sized, causing additional I/O or hard parses.
The value of parameter "sga_target" was "262144 M" during the analysis period.

   Recommendation 1: Database Configuration
   Estimated benefit is .45 active sessions, .62% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Increase the size of the SGA by setting the parameter "sga_target" to
      294912 M.

   Symptoms That Led to the Finding:
   ---------------------------------
      Wait class "User I/O" was consuming significant database time.
      Impact is 11.66 active sessions, 16.14% of total activity.


Finding 9: PL/SQL Execution
Impact is 1.54 active sessions, 2.13% of total activity.
--------------------------------------------------------
PL/SQL execution consumed significant database time.

   Recommendation 1: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is 1.52 active sessions, 2.1% of total activity.
   ------------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS"
      of type "PACKAGE" and ID 7169. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for
      addition information.
   Rationale
      5493 seconds spent in executing PL/SQL
      "SYS.DBMS_SPACE_ADMIN.SEGMENT_NUMBER_BLOCKS" of type "PACKAGE" and ID
      7169.

   Recommendation 2: SQL Tuning
   Estimated benefit is .02 active sessions, .03% of total activity.
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Action
      Tune the entry point PL/SQL "CENDOC.SP_JKDA_ALL" of type "PROCEDURE" and
      ID 3202007. Refer to the PL/SQL documentation for addition information.

Wait class "Application" was not consuming significant database time.
Wait class "Commit" was not consuming significant database time.
CPU was not a bottleneck for the instance.
Wait class "Network" was not consuming significant database time.
The network latency of the cluster interconnect was within acceptable limits
of 1 milliseconds.
Session connect and disconnect calls were not consuming significant database
time.
Hard parsing of SQL statements was not consuming significant database time.

Global Cache Transfer Statistics

  • Immediate (Immed) - Block Transfer NOT impacted by Remote Processing Delays
  • Busy (Busy) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote Contention
  • Congested (Congst) - Block Transfer impacted by Remote System Load
  • ordered by CR + Current Blocks Received desc

Back to Top

Interconnect Ping Latency Statistics

  • Ping latency of the roundtrip of a message from this instance to
  • target instances.
  • The target instance is identified by an instance number.
  • Average and standard deviation of ping latency is given in microseconds
  • for message sizes of 500 bytes and 8K.
  • Note that latency of a message from the instance to itself is used as
  • control, since message latency can include wait for CPU
Target Instance500B Ping CountAvg Latency 500B msgStddev 500B msg8K Ping CountAvg Latency 8K msgStddev 8K msg
